data.title: A 100-year-old Dow Theory triggers buy signal, indicating bullish market trend, reinforcing optimism for 3-year bull market.
data.description: The article discusses the recent activation of a rare buy signal based on the century-old Dow Theory in the market, indicating a positive outlook for the ongoing bull market in 2026. Adam Turnquist from LPL Financial noted that a Dow Theory buy signal has emerged, indicating that the primary trend for the market is upward. This theory, established by Charles Dow, uses the Dow Jones Industrial Average and the Dow Jones Transportation Average to identify market trends. The signal is triggered when industrial and transportation stocks rally together, suggesting improving economic conditions and a healthy market trend. This historical theory shows that when both sectors move in unison, it signifies a bullish market. This recent activation of the buy signal indicates that the market may still have room for growth in the near future.

data.title: JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon visited Palantir's office in D.C., nature of engagement unknown. - Benzinga.
data.description: JPMorgan Chase & Co. CEO Jamie Dimon paid a visit to Palantir Technologies' office in Washington, D.C. The purpose of the meeting remains undisclosed, however, Palantir confirmed Dimon's presence and the subsequent conversation through a social media update. The post expressed gratitude to Dimon for his visit, featuring pictures of him alongside Palantir's defense head Mike Gallagher. This interaction was acknowledged by Palantir on their social media account. The complete story can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Iranian crisis threatens Islamic Republic under Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. WTI crude rises to $62 amid oil market anxiety over potential supply disruption and U.S. military action.
data.description: The ongoing crisis in Iran has escalated into a severe political and economic turmoil, posing a significant threat to the Islamic Republic led by Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. The unrest, initially sparked by protests against soaring inflation and the devaluation of the Iranian rial, has evolved into the broadest anti-government demonstration since the 1979 revolution, causing concerns in global markets. The West Texas Intermediate crude oil surged for the sixth consecutive day on Wednesday, reaching $62 per barrel, a level unseen in three months. Traders are factoring in increased risks due to potential disruptions in oil supply and the looming possibility of U.S. military involvement. Financial analysts attribute the price hike to uncertainties surrounding Iran's future and the fear of Western military interference, which could impact exports and vital shipping routes. For the full article, please refer to Benzinga.com.

data.title: Trump supports Credit Card Competition Act to break Visa and Mastercard duopoly. 84% US market share, $223B revenue at stake. Visa and Mastercard shares drop 4.5% and 3.8% respectively. Swipe fees at 2.35%. Trump also calls for 10% cap on interest rates.
data.description: President Donald Trump has shown support for the Credit Card Competition Act, which aims to break the near-duopoly held by Visa and Mastercard. The proposal would require large banks to allow credit card transactions through at least two unaffiliated networks. This caused shares of Visa and Mastercard to plummet by 4.5% and 3.8% respectively, marking their worst drop in over six months. Visa and Mastercard currently dominate 84% of the U.S. credit card volume, generating an estimated $223 billion in annual revenue. The legislation also aims to reduce swipe fees paid by merchants, which were at 2.35% per credit card transaction in 2024. Trump had previously called for a one-year cap on credit card interest rates at 10%, emphasizing consumer protection and affordability. Goldman Sachs has raised concerns about potential risks to Visa and Mastercard.

data.title: Atossa Therapeutics gets FDA orphan drug status for (Z)-Endoxifen for Duchenne Muscular Dystrophy, bolstering rare disease program.
data.description: Atossa Therapeutics, Inc. (Nasdaq: ATOS) has received additional support for its (Z)-Endoxifen program targeting a rare pediatric neuromuscular disease. This designation complements the Rare Pediatric Disease Designation previously granted. The announcement was made on January 16, 2026 in Seattle. Atossa, a clinical-stage company, continues to advance its efforts in this area.
